message:
I was halfway through texting my sister directions to our house during last month's outage when my phone just… died. Like, *completely* dead. I'd been using the flashlight feature for 20 minutes and totally spaced on my battery percentage. Cue five minutes of panic in the dark, followed by me rooting around the junk drawer for a power bank I *thought* I had somewhere.

Turned out I did have one—dead as a doornail, naturally.

Now I keep a charged power bank in our emergency drawer, right next to the flashlights. Because staying connected during a power outage isn't just about convenience; it's about being able to reach people, get information, and not feel quite so isolated when the lights go out.

Little shifts like that have made a real difference in how ready we actually feel.

Do you have a go-to way you keep your phone alive when the power's out?
